mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases duration-sensitive valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% on 2016-02-01 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or positive 20d returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand that cascades into reduced corporate earnings forecasts. This interaction with payroll growth determines equity risk premium compression, predicting negative 20-day returns when both signals deteriorate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in fewer than 52% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines below 100 indicates supply-side contraction that signals recession risk. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premium widens in response to growth concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.85 (revised to 99.56) on 2016-01-01 shows measurement volatility
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.01 (i.e., near zero or positive) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5d returns exceed 60% frequency when INDPRO < 100.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,100 thousand units annualized signal contraction in forward-looking construction demand and residential investment. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as multiplier effects reduce aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,099 thousand on 2016-01-01 near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and forward 60d returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is above 0.25% indicates monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation, constraining real money growth. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive returns in low-inflation regimes as policy accommodation is implicit.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand persons month-over-month signals labor market momentum loss that precedes earnings downgrades. This always-on mechanism predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as forward earnings expectations compress.
regime_hint: any regime with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns exceed 62% frequency when monthly PAYEMS growth < 100 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary demand weakness. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions and predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as terminal rate expectations shift lower.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,455.1B (revised to 18,892.2B) on 2015-10-01 shows significant revision risk
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in > 56% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60d returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
